scope.notes: 20+ FR files affect: .claude/agents/, .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md, .claude/rules/, .aiox-core/development/agents/, .synapse/metrics/, .aiox/gate-logs/, enforce-*.cjs hooks. Estimated 20-30 files touched (cross-cutting concerns across agent system, gate system, and observability layer).

integration.notes: 4 workflows (SDC, QA Loop, Spec Pipeline, Brownfield) tested end-to-end. 12 agents interacting. Registry routing required. Gate logging (JSONL). CodeRabbit self-healing integration. Multiple data flows between agents and gate layers.

infrastructure.notes: New .aiox/gate-logs/ JSONL logging infrastructure. Possible cache layer for context loading (NFR-1.3 >80%). Dependencies: gate-logger.cjs, agent-activation-tracker.cjs, session-manager.js. Mostly configuration, some new service patterns.

knowledge.notes: AIOX framework well-documented. Gate patterns exist (EPIC-9 reference). But: Spec Pipeline Phase 2-4 (assess/research/critique) are NEW patterns not in production yet. New critique/revision loop cycle (Phase 7-8) is novel. Team knows agents, but Spec Pipeline process itself is unproven operationally.

risk.notes: Core framework (agent system, constitutional gates Art. I-VII). Affects all 12 agents. If gates fail, determinism compromised. High user impact (internal AIOX team + future deployments). But: High reversibility (L4 stories only, can rollback). No user-facing risk (framework-only).

pipelinePhases: 3. Research — Deep dive into Spec Pipeline precedents, gate patterns
4. Write Spec — Full specification document with implementation strategy
5. Critique v1 — QA gate: @qa validates spec completeness
7. Critique v2 — Final QA gate: no blockers
8. Plan — Implementation roadmap (epic breakdown into 12.1-12.12 stories)
